normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU1_bst_precondition {t : Tree} (h : BST t) : BST t := h
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Benchmark-facing: the input tree must satisfy the BST property (strict in-order ascending).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_bst_invariant_preservation (t : Tree) (key : i32) (hbst : BST t) : BST (bst_insert t key)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Inserting a key into a valid BST yields a tree that is still a valid BST.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_keyset_change_absent (t : Tree) (key : i32) (hbst : BST t) (hnot : ¬ Mem t key) : KeyMultiset (bst_insert t key) = KeyMultiset t + {key}
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key is absent, insertion adds exactly one occurrence of the key to the key multiset.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_keyset_change_present (t : Tree) (key : i32) (hbst : BST t) (hin : Mem t key) : KeyMultiset (bst_insert t key) = KeyMultiset t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the key is already present, insertion is a multiset no-op.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_duplicate_noop (t : Tree) (key : i32) (hbst : BST t) (hin : Mem t key) : KeyMultiset (bst_insert t key) = KeyMultiset t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Benchmark-facing duplicate behavior: inserting an existing key does not change the key multiset.
